title: Minimal Permutation-Invariant Qudit Codes from Edge-Colorings of Complete Graphs
abstract: We study permutation-invariant quantum codes in the symmetric subspace $\mathrm{Sym}^n(\mathbb{C}^q) $ of $n$ qudits of local dimension $q$. For every integer $q\geq 2$, we construct a permutation-invariant code with parameters $((4,q,2))_q$. Thus four physical qudits suffice to encode one logical qudit with distance two in the symmetric sector for every local dimension. We also show, using linear-programming constraints for permutation-invariant quantum codes, that no permutation-invariant code of dimension $q$ and distance at least $2$ exists in $\mathrm{Sym}^n(\mathbb{C}^q)$ for $n\leq 3$. Hence four qudits are necessary and sufficient.   The construction has a simple representation-theoretic and combinatorial description. In the irreducible $\mathrm{SU}(q)$-module $\mathrm{Sym}^4(\mathbb{C}^q)$, the distance-two Knill-Laflamme conditions split into root and Cartan parts. By restricting supports to the even-entry occupation layer, all root-error conditions vanish automatically. The remaining Cartan conditions reduce to linear balancing constraints on packets of occupation vectors. These packets admit a natural graph-theoretic interpretation in terms of the vertices and edges of the complete graph $K_q$: for odd $q$, they are organized by the midpoint rule, while for even $q$, they are organized by a decomposition of $K_q$ into perfect matchings. In this way, the existence of minimal $((4,q,2))_q$ permutation-invariant codes is reduced to a parity-dependent edge-coloring problem on $K_q$.
summary: This paper establishes that four physical qudits are both necessary and sufficient to encode one logical qudit with distance two within the symmetric subspace. The authors provide a universal construction for any dimension q by mapping the quantum error-correction requirements to combinatorial edge-coloring problems on complete graphs.
main_problem: Determining the minimum block length n required to construct permutation-invariant (PI) quantum codes that can encode one logical qudit of dimension q with distance d=2.
main_result: The authors prove that n=4 is the minimum block length required and provide a general construction for ((4, q, 2))_q codes for all q >= 2 using graph-theoretic edge-colorings.
method: The study uses representation theory of SU(q), linear programming constraints, and a combinatorial reduction of the Knill-Laflamme conditions to edge-coloring problems on complete graphs.
model_or_system: The system consists of n qudits of local dimension q restricted to the symmetric subspace (permutation-invariant subspace) of the total Hilbert space.
key_observables: Knill-Laflamme error-detection conditions, weight/occupation numbers, and Cartan/root operator expectation values.
important_parameters: Local dimension q, block length n, code distance d=2, and code dimension K=q.
main_assumptions: The codes are restricted to the symmetric subspace and are designed specifically to detect single-site (one-body) traceless errors.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the problem of PI codes, uses linear programming to prove the non-existence of codes for n <= 3, then develops a construction for n=4 by decomposing error conditions into root and Cartan parts, and finally uses graph theory (edge-coloring and 1-factorization) to provide explicit constructions for both odd and even q.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for quantum error correction in systems with inherent symmetries, such as trapped ions or Rydberg atoms, where permutation-invariant noise models are physically motivated.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
